title: tillaea umbellata
intro: Tillaea umbellata, also known as the False Pimpernel, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Haloragaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antiviral activity. Tillaea umbellata can be used in herbal preparations like infusions, tinctures, and salves, making it a valuable resource in natural medicine.
